fileOperations.contentDisplayed: The file content is displayed in the diff view above

interactive.title: Interactive Prompt
interactive.waiting: Waiting for your response in the CLI
interactive.instruction: Please select an option in your terminal where Claude is running.
interactive.instructionDetail: In the CLI, you would select this option interactively using arrow keys or by typing the number.

permissions.retry: Permission saved. Retry the request to use the tool.
permissions.error: Unable to update permissions. Please try again.

todo.updated: Todo list has been updated successfully

usageLimit.resetAt: Claude usage limit reached. Your limit will reset at **{{time}} {{timezone}}** - {{date}}

descriptions.default: Only trusted commands (ls, cat, grep, git status, etc.) run automatically. Other commands are skipped. Can write to workspace.
descriptions.acceptEdits: All commands run automatically within the workspace. Full auto mode with sandboxed execution.
descriptions.bypassPermissions: Full system access with no restrictions. All commands run automatically with full disk and network access. Use with caution.
descriptions.plan: Planning mode - no commands are executed

gemini.description: Control how Gemini CLI handles operation approvals.

default.title: Standard (Ask for Approval)
default.description: Gemini will prompt for approval before executing commands, writing files, and fetching web resources.

autoEdit.title: Auto Edit (Skip File Approvals)
autoEdit.description: Gemini will automatically approve file edits and web fetches, but will still prompt for shell commands.

yolo.title: YOLO (Bypass All Permissions)
yolo.description: Gemini will execute all operations without asking for approval. Exercise caution.

input.placeholder: Type / for commands, @ for files, or ask {{provider}} anything...

hintText.ctrlEnter: Ctrl+Enter to send • Shift+Enter for new line • Tab to change modes • / for slash commands
hintText.enter: Enter to send • Shift+Enter for new line • Tab to change modes • / for slash commands

input.clickToChangeMode: Click to change permission mode (or press Tab in input)

selector.title: Thinking Mode
selector.description: Extended thinking gives Claude more time to evaluate alternatives
selector.tip: Higher thinking modes take more time but provide more thorough analysis

providerSelection.title: Choose Your AI Assistant
providerSelection.description: Select a provider to start a new conversation

readyPrompt.claude: Ready to use Claude with {{model}}. Start typing your message below.
readyPrompt.cursor: Ready to use Cursor with {{model}}. Start typing your message below.
readyPrompt.codex: Ready to use Codex with {{model}}. Start typing your message below.
readyPrompt.gemini: Ready to use Gemini with {{model}}. Start typing your message below.
readyPrompt.default: Select a provider above to begin

continue.title: Continue your conversation
continue.description: Ask questions about your code, request changes, or get help with development tasks

messages.perfWarning: All messages loaded — scrolling may be slower. Click "Scroll to bottom" to restore performance.

selectProject.title: Select a Project
selectProject.description: Choose a project to open an interactive shell in that directory

projectSelection.startChatWithProvider: Select a project to start chatting with {{provider}}

conversationMode.toggleTitle: Switch between chat mode and Harness flow
conversationMode.disabledNoClaude: This project has not enabled the shared .claude workspace system. Only regular chat is available.
conversationMode.bannerHarness: Harness flow is enabled. Team workflow commands will run through the shared Harness pipeline.
conversationMode.bannerChatAvailable: You are in chat mode. Switch to Harness flow to run the shared team workflow commands.
conversationMode.commandRequiresHarness: This is a team workflow command. Switch to Harness flow before running it.
conversationMode.freeformBlocked: The current Harness entry accepts shared team workflow commands. Use a supported workflow command to continue.

currentTask.empty: There is no active Harness task in this project yet. Switch the chat area to Harness flow and run a team workflow command first.

commands.empty: No Harness commands are available for this project.

artifacts.empty: No artifacts have been scanned yet.

runs.empty: There are no main / reviewer / validator run records yet.

checkpoint.empty: This task has no checkpoint yet.

feedback.empty: There is no feedback record for this project yet.

bootstrap.empty: You can upgrade a project without .claude into a Harness-enabled project from here.

workspace.title: Workspace Status
workspace.helpLine1: This is the project-level status strip.
workspace.helpLine2: Use it to check whether Harness is available, whether Prime is stale, which task is active, and which stage the task is currently in.

currentTask.title: Current Task
currentTask.helpLine1: This section shows the Harness task currently running for the project.
currentTask.helpLine2: If you want to know which stage is blocked, whether the task is fresh or blocked, or whether the gate is stuck, start here.

packs.title: Pack Versions
packs.helpLine1: Packs are temporary handoff bundles for sub-agents.
packs.helpLine2: This section shows which pack versions reviewer and validator are reading, and whether each pack is fresh, stale, or invalidated.

commands.title: Command Registry
commands.helpLine1: This is the shared command registry.
commands.helpLine2: It tells you which commands exist in the project, which stage they belong to, what their preconditions are, and which artifacts they write.

timeline.title: Timeline
timeline.helpLine1: This is the task execution timeline.
timeline.helpLine2: Use it to answer who did what, when it happened, which pack version was used, and why the step passed or failed.

artifacts.title: Artifact Workbench
artifacts.helpLine1: This is the long-term artifact workbench.
artifacts.helpLine2: Plans, reviews, reports, and feedback belong here. Artifacts are long-term truth; Packs are temporary handoffs.

gate.title: Gate Details
gate.helpLine1: The gate is the final pass/fail checkpoint.
gate.helpLine2: Review, validation, and human decision are summarized here. If any lane fails or is invalidated, the task is not truly done.

runs.title: Recent Runs
runs.helpLine1: This shows the recent real runs for main, reviewer, and validator.
runs.helpLine2: Use it to trace who ran, at which stage, how the model was resolved, and what conclusion came back.

benchmark.title: Benchmark Dashboard
benchmark.helpLine1: This is the quantitative evaluation panel.
benchmark.helpLine2: It exists to prove whether Harness actually improves AI coding outcomes instead of relying on intuition. It shows datasets, results, and metrics such as M19-M27.

checkpoint.title: Checkpoint / Resume
checkpoint.helpLine1: A checkpoint is a task snapshot.
checkpoint.helpLine2: You can save the task state at a point in time and resume it later so long-running tasks do not lose all progress.

feedback.title: Knowledge Feedback
feedback.helpLine1: This area stores experience that has been proven useful.
feedback.helpLine2: It does not directly rewrite the official rules. Instead it saves the learned experience as candidate knowledge first, then lets you decide whether it should be promoted.

bootstrap.title: Bootstrap / Template
bootstrap.helpLine2: If a project does not have the shared .claude workspace system yet, use this to generate a minimal skeleton and let it enter the Harness workflow.

help.seedDatasetLine1: A seed dataset is a fixed batch of benchmark sample tasks.
help.seedDatasetLine2: It is not your business data. It is the benchmark starting point used to compare baseline and Harness runs over time.
help.runSeedEvalLine1: This runs an evaluation on the current dataset.
help.runSeedEvalLine2: After it finishes you can see whether pass rate, first-pass validation rate, and hallucination rate have met the target thresholds.
help.createCheckpoint: Save the current task, gate, pack, and timestamp as a recoverable snapshot.
help.writeFeedback: Write verified experience from the current task as a reusable knowledge feedback record.
help.initProject: Generate a minimal .claude skeleton for the current project so it can move from normal chat into Harness.
